subject: Contract renewal — Lowlands Dairy
body:
Hi Pieter,

Happy new year. As you know, our contract with Lowlands expired December 31. We're operating on a month-to-month basis for now, but I'd like to get the renewal discussion started properly. Can we schedule a call this month?

David

subject: RE: Contract renewal — Lowlands Dairy
body:
David,

Good to hear from you. Yes, renewal is on our radar too. Before we go to formal terms, I need to flag that our production planning team has been reviewing our outbound commitments across all customers and we see some changes needed on the volume side. Happy to discuss on a call — how does January 22 look?

Pieter

subject: Renewal framework — Lowlands Dairy proposal
body:
David,

Good call earlier. As discussed, I'm sending over our proposed framework for the 2026 contract:

- Term: 18 months
- Volume: minimum 25 MT/quarter, maximum 40 MT/quarter
- Price: €3,350/ton
- Payment: Net 30

The key change from the old contract is the minimum commitment — we've moved it to 25 MT from 20 MT. This reflects our new production scheduling requirements. We can't hold capacity for a customer at 20 MT minimum anymore, it simply doesn't work for our co-op members.

I've cc'd Anja Dekker, our volume planning lead, who can answer any questions on the supply side.

Look forward to your feedback.
Pieter

subject: RE: Renewal framework — Lowlands Dairy proposal
body:
Pieter,

Thanks for the framework. A few reactions:

On price — €3,350 is a significant step up from the old contract. We'd want to discuss this more. Can you share what's driving that number?

On the minimum volume increase — 25 MT vs the previous 20 MT is something I need to check internally. Our Q4 2025 actual offtake was around 19 MT, so committing to a 25 MT floor is not straightforward for us.

I'll come back to you with a counter by end of next week.

David

subject: Q1 delivery — January/February
body:
David,

This is Anja from the Lowlands planning team. Just flagging that while renewal is being discussed, we are continuing to process your standing monthly orders under the old terms. The January shipment of 7 MT left on January 31, ETA at your site February 6.

For February we have you down for another 7 MT around February 20 — please confirm this is still needed.

Anja

subject: RE: Q1 delivery — January/February
body:
Confirmed, February 7 MT on the 20th is fine. Thanks Anja.

David

subject: Counter-proposal — renewal terms
body:
Pieter,

Here is our counter on the renewal framework:

- Term: 18 months (agreed)
- Volume: minimum 20 MT/quarter (we want to hold the original minimum), maximum 40 MT/quarter (agreed)
- Price: €3,250/ton (midpoint between your proposal and the old contract price)
- Payment: Net 30 (agreed)

The 20 MT minimum is important for us — our demand profile can be variable and we need the flexibility. We're happy to commit to best-efforts to hit 25+ MT when demand allows, but we can't contractually floor it there.

Let me know your thoughts.

David

subject: RE: Counter-proposal — renewal terms
body:
David,

Thank you for the counter. On price — we could consider €3,280 but not lower, our input costs simply don't allow it.

On volume minimum — I hear you on the flexibility argument but 20 MT is really not workable for us. The co-op voted on this. We would be willing to consider 23 MT as a compromise minimum.

Still some gap to close. Can we get on a call later this week?

Pieter

subject: Following up — renewal status
body:
Pieter,

We've been going back and forth since January and are still not aligned on the two key items — price and minimum volume. I want to make sure we don't drift further on an informal basis without a signed contract.

Where do things stand from your side? Are you still at 23 MT minimum / €3,280?

David

subject: RE: Following up — renewal status
body:
David,

Yes, we are still at those positions. I've also had our legal team put together a draft contract document. Sending separately — please review and let us know your comments. We'd like to have something signed before end of April if possible.

Pieter

subject: Draft contract review — open items
body:
Pieter,

We've reviewed the draft. Our legal team has flagged a few things. The main issues remain the same: minimum volume (we reviewed internally and 20 MT is our limit — we can't move on this) and the price (we can go to €3,260 but not €3,280).

I know we've been at an impasse on these for a while. Is there any flexibility on your side, or do you need to go back to the co-op?

David

subject: Q2 delivery schedule
body:
David,

Just sending over the Q2 delivery schedule as we are continuing on the informal basis:
- April 28: 7 MT
- May 19: 7 MT
- June 10: 6 MT

Total Q2: 20 MT. Please confirm if these dates work.

Note from Pieter: the renewal discussion is still ongoing, he will follow up separately.

Anja

subject: Renewal — still open
body:
David,

I owe you an update. I brought the 20 MT minimum back to our board and they will not approve it. The co-op's position is firm at 23 MT. On price we could meet at €3,270.

I know this is frustrating. We have been very patient operating without a signed contract but this can't continue indefinitely. What is Strauss's final position?

Pieter
